zone_section_start.title_label_raw: RDD ZONE: RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT ZONE

zone_class_reference.summary: RDD is an established class of use zone.

permitted_uses.use_name: R-1 and R-2 uses as hereinbefore set out

prohibitions.summary: No development for any purpose other than one or more of the uses set out in subsection 62A(1).
No land or building use in whole or in part for any purpose other than one or more of the uses set out in subsection 62A(1).

incorporated_use_requirements.summary: Buildings erected, altered or used for R-1 and R-2 uses in an RDD Zone shall comply with the requirements of the R-1 and R-2 Zones respectively.

development_agreement_controls.condition_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this by-law, Council may, by resolution, approve any specific development application pursuant to Policy 1.5.1 of Part II, Section X (Schedule I) of the Municipal Planning Strategy.

controls.condition_text: A legal description of the total site proposed for development, including present and proposed ownership.
A development schedule indicating the stages or phases of development from beginning to completion.

Total number and type of dwelling units, lot size, proposed lot coverages, approximate gross and net population densities, total amount of open space and usable open space, and total amount of commercial and, where applicable, institutional facilities.

Site plan and supporting maps describing existing topographic conditions including contours at 5 foot intervals, water courses or bodies, flood plains, wetlands, significant natural features and forest cover including the general location of trees 6 inches in diameter or greater, proposed lot lines, and the location and size of all existing and proposed buildings and structures including maximum heights, dwelling unit types, density per type, and non-residential structures.

The location and size of all areas to be dedicated or reserved as common open spaces, public parks, recreation areas, and other public uses; existing and proposed street circulation systems including parking and serviced areas and major points of ingress and egress; existing and proposed pedestrian circulation systems and their interrelationship with the vehicular circulation system; existing and proposed public utility systems and, where applicable, sanitary and water systems on and adjacent to the development site; and a general schematic landscape plan with adjacent-area context information.
Any other information required to evaluate the impact of the proposed development.

spatial_features_needed.reason: Defines the total site proposed for development under subsection 62B(2).
Controls development applications approved under subsection 62B(1).
